steps.title: Apply the range pattern
steps.content: Earlier research found that narrow-range plant species tend to be more genetically homogeneous than species with extensive ranges. M. klissingiana begins with a narrow range in Tamaulipas, and the simulation has it spreading to new habitats.

steps.title: Predict the genetic change
steps.content: Choice A concerns whether other species could survive in Tamaulipas. Choice C addresses species in neighboring states, and Choice D concerns abundance of other species. If M. klissingiana becomes more widely distributed, the prior pattern predicts less genetic homogeneity over time. The answer is B.
